skill.name: image-enhancer
skill.description: Improves the quality of images, especially screenshots, by enhancing resolution, sharpness, and clarity. Perfect for preparing images for presentations, documentation, or social media posts.
skill.summary: Enhance screenshots and images for clearer presentation, documentation, and social publishing.
skill.author: davila7

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported Ruby backtick execution and weak cryptography, but review found only Markdown fences, examples, and descriptive text. No executable code, prompt injection, network access, credential access, or weak cryptographic implementation was found.

low_findings.title: Dismissed Static Finding: Markdown Code Fences
low_findings.description: The external command findings point to Markdown code fences and natural language prompt examples, not shell execution. The examples describe image enhancement requests and do not execute commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged lines are Markdown triple-backtick delimiters around examples and output text. There is no Ruby code, shell interpolation, or command execution syntax.

low_findings.title: Dismissed Static Finding: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography finding points to the front matter description. It contains image enhancement text and no hash, cipher, key handling, or cryptographic operation.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 3 is a plain description of image quality improvement. No cryptographic algorithm name or implementation is present.

content.value_statement: Low-quality screenshots can make documentation, presentations, and social posts harder to read. This skill guides Claude, Codex, or Claude Code through practical image improvement steps.
actual_capabilities: Analyzes image resolution, format, sharpness, and compression artifacts.
Suggests upscaling, sharpening, cleanup, and output format choices.
Supports screenshot improvement for blogs, documentation, and presentations.
Can guide batch enhancement of image folders when tools are available.
Recommends platform-specific sizing for web, print, and social media.
limitations: The skill is instructional and does not include executable image processing code.
Actual enhancement depends on available local tools or model image capabilities.
Severely blurred or damaged images may not recover fine detail.
Batch workflows require user approval before modifying many files.

use_cases.title: Prepare Documentation Screenshots
use_cases.description: Improve UI screenshots so text, icons, and controls remain readable in technical documentation.

use_cases.title: Polish Presentation Images
use_cases.description: Upscale and sharpen images before placing them on slides or large displays.

use_cases.title: Optimize Social Images
use_cases.description: Adapt image quality and sizing for publishing on LinkedIn, Instagram, or other social channels.

prompt_templates.title: Enhance One Image
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for a single screenshot or image.
prompt_templates.prompt: Improve the quality of screenshot.png while preserving the original file.

prompt_templates.title: Fix Blurry Screenshot
prompt_templates.scenario: Improve readability for a specific problem.
prompt_templates.prompt: Sharpen this blurry screenshot and make the text easier to read for documentation.

prompt_templates.title: Prepare Social Post
prompt_templates.scenario: Optimize an image for a publishing target.
prompt_templates.prompt: Enhance this image for a LinkedIn post and recommend the best output size and format.

prompt_templates.title: Batch Improve Folder
prompt_templates.prompt: Review all PNG files in this folder, enhance only the low-quality ones, and keep originals as backups.

output_examples.input: Improve the quality of a product screenshot for a blog post.
output_examples.output: The assistant identifies slight blur, recommends upscaling, sharpens interface edges, and saves an enhanced copy for publication.

output_examples.input: Prepare a compressed JPEG for a presentation slide.
output_examples.output: The assistant reduces visible artifacts, improves contrast, and suggests a higher-resolution export for large screens.

output_examples.input: Enhance all images in a folder for documentation.
output_examples.output: The assistant reviews the images, reports which files need changes, and creates improved copies while keeping originals.

best_practices: Keep original image files before applying enhancement steps.
State the target use case, such as web, print, slides, or social media.
Review enhanced images for text readability and visual artifacts before publishing.
anti_patterns: Do not overwrite source images without a backup.
Do not upscale very small images far beyond their useful detail.
Do not apply aggressive sharpening that creates halos or distorted text.

faq.question: Does this skill include image editing software?
faq.answer: No. It provides workflow guidance and depends on available image tools or model capabilities.

faq.question: Can it improve screenshots for documentation?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill is designed for clearer screenshots, readable text, and publication-ready documentation assets.

faq.question: Will it preserve original files?
faq.answer: The skill recommends preserving originals and saving enhanced versions as separate files.

faq.question: Can it process many images at once?
faq.answer: Yes, it can guide batch processing when the environment has suitable image tools.

faq.question: Can it recover missing image detail?
faq.answer: No. It can improve clarity, but it cannot recreate detail that is not present.

faq.question: Is it safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes. The reviewed skill contains documentation only and no executable code or confirmed dangerous behavior.
